pi_items.source: Verified against export_result.sh TARGET_FOLDER_NAME and verifier.py logic.
Verified against setup_task.sh python ground truth generation script and export_result.sh.
Verified against expected_recipient parameter in verifier.py.
Verified against case_ref parameter in verifier.py.

privileged_info_summary: The dataset used for this task is the real-world SpamAssassin Public Corpus. A critical logic flaw exists in the verification system: the task explicitly instructs the agent to search both Inbox and Junk folders and move ONLY emails containing specific keywords ('license', 'copyright', 'patent', 'GPL'). However, exact programmatic analysis confirms there are absolutely zero matching emails within the generated Junk (Spam) dataset, while exactly 2 matches exist in the Inbox (ham_038.eml, ham_042.eml). Despite this truth, the verifier explicitly deducts 10 points for failing the 'Cross-folder Search' criterion if zero Junk emails are preserved. An agent perfectly executing the instructions will incorrectly lose points unless it intentionally preserves a non-matching Junk email to trigger the false positive check. All metadata properties (target_folder, keywords, recipient, case_reference) perfectly match the underlying export and verification scripts.
